channel.title: Zaher Karp, Writing
channel.description: Healthcare data engineering, Medicare Advantage analytics, and adjacent things.

item.title: The Inversion: When Developer Infrastructure Breaks Under Its Own Momentum
item.description: Stack Overflow visits collapsed while GitHub commits surged, and the platform underneath both is degrading in real time. The productivity gains from AI coding agents are real. So is the load they put on infrastructure that wasn't designed for them.

item.title: Two States, One Pathogen: A Browser-Side Stochastic SEIRV Simulator
item.description: Methodology and intuition behind the browser-based SEIRV epidemic simulator on this site. Poisson tau-leaping, state-level coverage, and why the ribbon matters more than any single run.

item.title: The Slowest Way to Learn Web Design Is the Only Way That Worked for Me
item.description: A personal history of building and rebuilding a website across three eras, from Squarespace drag-and-drop to a pure HTML and CSS rebuild, and what the increasing friction of each step actually taught me.

item.title: How the Stars Cliff Simulator Works
item.description: A short explainer for the Stars Cliff Simulator: why ordinal logistic regression is the right tool for a 1–5 star outcome, how P(clearing 4.0★) falls out of the model for free, and what the distance-to-cliff readout actually measures.

item.title: The Stars Cliff Simulator: Methodology & Evidence
item.description: The statistical methodology and published literature behind the Stars Cliff Simulator — a public, teaching-oriented ordinal logistic regression tool focused on the 4.0★ Quality Bonus Payment threshold.

item.title: Two Ways to Build a Web App (and What You’re Actually Choosing)
item.description: Most applications boil down to server-rendered or API-driven designs. The difference isn’t just technical, it shapes how your system evolves.

item.title: The ECDS Shock Index: Modeling Distribution Risk in Medicare Advantage Stars
item.description: ECDS adoption is not just a data modernization effort. It introduces measurable distribution risk into the Stars ecosystem.

item.title: Concurrency: The Story We Tell vs. the System We Run
item.description: Concurrency sounds like “do more at once.” In real data systems it’s mostly coordination under constraint, and in healthcare analytics the constraints include trust, auditability, and deadlines.

item.title: Why Pull Requests Changed Everything in Healthcare Data Science
item.description: How version control and pull requests transformed healthcare analytics from chaotic file management into transparent, collaborative systems that save lives.

item.title: Why You Probably Shouldn't Version Your Fact Table
item.description: Most teams version their fact tables because they've modeled their dimensions incorrectly. Understanding the true grain of your table—whether it's event-based or state-based—will save you from double-counting metrics, failed audits, and painful refactoring projects in healthcare data warehouses.

item.title: Debugging GitHub Actions: The Heredoc Horror Story
item.description: A deep dive into debugging a GitHub Actions workflow where heredoc syntax clashed with YAML parsing, leading to five different errors and one elegant solution.

item.title: LLM Inference Is Not Just Bigger Inference
item.description: Why serving large language models is a fundamentally different systems problem from traditional ML inference, especially in applied healthcare IT.

item.title: What LLM Systems Teach Healthcare IT About Architecture
item.description: Large language models force healthcare IT teams to rethink batching, memory, routing, and state management at production scale.

item.title: Security Starts With the Problem You’re Trying to Solve
item.description: A brief description of your blog post (1-2 sentences)

item.title: Why CMS Never Let STAR Ratings Touch Meaningful Use
item.description: On the surface, it looks like an omission - but CMS understood that capability and outcomes cannot be governed the same way, even when one enables the other. The deliberate separation was probably the right call.

item.title: The Gravity of STAR Ratings
item.description: How outcomes shape programs they never appear in - STAR Ratings operate less like a measurement program and more like a financial force that influences clinical workflows, data governance, and analytics investments across healthcare.

item.title: America First, Global Health Last
item.description: A quantitative analysis of documented international health program disruptions, funding cuts, and their human toll during 2025

item.title: The Dismantling of America's Public Health Shield
item.description: A quantitative analysis of documented workforce reductions, budget cuts, and health outcomes in the U.S. public health system during 2025

item.title: We Were Bending the Cost Curve. Then We Looked Away.
item.description: For a brief, underappreciated period, Medicare and Medicaid showed real signs of cost stabilization. This post examines what changed, why it mattered, and how we quietly abandoned the lessons when the trajectory shifted.

item.title: From Lambda to Kappa: What Modern Data Architecture Really Means for Healthcare Tech
item.description: Why the shift away from Lambda architecture matters, what Kappa gets right (and wrong), and how healthcare APIs force us to be more pragmatic than dogmatic.

item.title: Kimball vs. Inmon in Healthcare Tech: Warehouses, Marts, and APIs Walk Into a Clinic
item.description: A practical, slightly opinionated take on Kimball and Inmon, viewed through healthcare data platforms, APIs, and the realities of shipping software.

item.title: Making PostgreSQL Connections More Resilient with TCP Keepalive
item.description: Why idle database connections fail, and how TCP keepalive tuning prevents silent disconnects in PostgreSQL.

item.title: I Don’t Actually Care About Making Change
item.description: Solving the same problem in Haskell, Python, and SQL reveals less about coins and more about how programming languages shape how we think about correctness, process, and structure.

item.title: Deletes Are Data Too
item.description: Deletes are the quiet failure mode in data pipelines. Here are the main ways to detect them and the tradeoffs you’re actually signing up for.

item.title: Publicly Available but Practically Unavailable
item.description: Why healthcare executives repeatedly ask for data that technically exists, yet remains inaccessible in usable form—and how misunderstanding 'public data' quietly undermines quality, cost, and governance decisions.

item.title: Weighting Provider Panels Across Primary Care
item.description: UW Health's innovative approach to patient empanelment using data-driven weighting methodologies that account for age, gender, and insurance factors.
